name: Slack
security: Verify Slack request signatures (v0) using SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET on every incoming Events API request.
Use bot tokens (xoxb-) for app actions; never expose user tokens (xoxp-).
Scope OAuth grants narrowly — chat:write is enough for most notifier bots.
Use Incoming Webhooks only for low-risk one-way notifications, not for sensitive flows.

patterns.name: Incoming webhook notifications
patterns.description: Post messages to a single channel via a webhook URL — simplest pattern for alerts.

patterns.name: Events API
patterns.description: Receive workspace events (messages, mentions, reactions) via subscribed event types.

patterns.name: Slash commands
patterns.description: Build /commands that POST to your endpoint and respond with messages.

triggers.name: Message in channel
triggers.description: Fires when a new message is posted in a channel the bot is in.

triggers.name: Bot mentioned
triggers.description: Fires when the bot is @-mentioned in a message.

triggers.name: Reaction added
triggers.description: Fires when an emoji reaction is added to a message.

triggers.name: Member joined channel
triggers.description: Fires when a user joins a channel the bot is in.

actions.name: Send message to channel
actions.description: Post a message to a Slack channel.

actions.name: Send direct message
actions.description: Send a DM to a user (opens an IM channel first).

actions.name: Reply in thread
actions.description: Post a message as a thread reply to a parent message.

actions.name: Create channel
actions.description: Create a new public or private channel.

actions.name: Add reaction
actions.description: Add an emoji reaction to a message.

auth.description: Bot user OAuth token (xoxb-…) for sending messages and using the Web API.
Signing secret used to verify incoming Slack Events API requests.
Incoming Webhook URL for posting to a single channel without OAuth.
